name: NNI (Microsoft Neural Network Intelligence)

performance.notes: Web UI overhead; supports local and remote training platforms; SMAC, TPE, BOHB

agent_readiness.notes: Microsoft's AutoML and HPO toolkit with web UI. nni.get_next_parameter() in trial code. nni.report_intermediate_result(val). nni.report_final_result(val). config.yaml defines search space and tuner. nnictl create --config config.yaml. Web UI at localhost:8080 for visualization. Tuners: TPE, SMAC, BOHB, CQA, Evolution, Metis. Neural Architecture Search (NAS): DARTs, ENAS. Model compression: pruning, quantization. Less community momentum than Optuna but more complete AutoML story.

gotchas: YAML config file required — more boilerplate than Optuna's Python API
Development activity has slowed vs Optuna/Ray Tune
Web UI dependency adds complexity for programmatic use
NAS features partially overlap with Microsoft's own tools
best_when: AutoML including Neural Architecture Search (DARTS, ENAS)
Teams wanting web UI for experiment visualization
Microsoft Research reproducibility (matching published NAS results)
avoid_when: Standard HPO — Optuna simpler and better maintained
Distributed HPO at scale — Ray Tune more scalable
Teams wanting Python-first API without YAML config
summary: NNI (Neural Network Intelligence) is Microsoft's AutoML toolkit combining HPO, NAS, and model compression. Trial code calls nni.get_next_parameter() and nni.report_final_result(). YAML config defines search space and tuner (TPE, SMAC, BOHB). nnictl create starts experiment with web UI at localhost:8080. Neural Architecture Search (DARTS, ENAS) and model compression (pruning, quantization) built-in. Development has slowed — prefer Optuna for HPO, dedicated NAS tools for architecture search.
